page: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors running at 2.0 GHz, configured as 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 188.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 3.91 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a 960 GB SSD, and the virtualization is managed via QEMU.

Benchmark evaluations place the ccx63 in the top 25% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, with scores of 3058.9 and 73650.13 respectively. It demonstrates strong capabilities in cryptography and database operations, achieving 64.55 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and scoring 22243.57 in PassMark database operations. Text generation for small LLM models reaches top-tier performance at 334.39 tokens per second, though prompt processing for larger 70B models is average at 12.32 tokens per second. Memory read bandwidth is average, measuring 154.31 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, while large block write bandwidth is strong at 95.71 GB/sec.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution speeds without resource contention, offering high resource density for multi-threaded tasks. However, the hardware lacks AVX-512 vector extensions, which may limit performance in specific scientific computing workloads. Given the balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong database and compilation benchmarks, the ccx63 is positioned for database hosting, software compilation pipelines, and low-latency web serving.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 188.0 GB of RAM and 960 GB of local SSD storage. The instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng and PassMark database benchmarks, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. While memory read bandwidth and larger LLM inference speeds remain average, its dedicated CPU allocation provides reliable, contention-free execution. Lacking AVX-512 extensions, it is best utilized for database operations, software compilation, and multi-threaded general-purpose workloads.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 48 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 188 GB RAM, and 960 GB SSD storage, delivering strong database and multi-core processing performance.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance with forty-eight vCPUs and local SSD storage for consistent multi-threaded processing and database workloads.
